prompt:

Target declarations: CausalSmith.Substrate.BernsteinSzegoTrig.szegoInterp_hasDerivAt, CausalSmith.Substrate.BernsteinSzegoTrig.szegoInterp_amplitude

## Instructions
Work in the Lean 4 / Mathlib file `/<repo-root>/CausalSmith/CausalSmith/Substrate/BernsteinSzegoTrig/Interp.lean`. Replace the two `sorry`s (and ONLY those) — do not change any theorem statement/signature, do not add axioms, do not weaken. Verify with `lake build CausalSmith.Substrate.BernsteinSzegoTrig.Interp` (run from `/<repo-root>/CausalSmith`); success = 0 errors, and the only remaining `sorry` warnings in the project are in OTHER files.

Definition in the file: `szegoInterp β Q₀ Q₁ t₀ t = Q₀ * Real.cos ((β:ℝ)*(t-t₀)) + (Q₁/(β:ℝ)) * Real.sin ((β:ℝ)*(t-t₀))`.

(1) `szegoInterp_hasDerivAt (β:ℕ) (hβ : 1 ≤ β) (Q₀ Q₁ t₀ : ℝ) : HasDerivAt (fun t => szegoInterp β Q₀ Q₁ t₀ t) Q₁ t₀`.
Roadmap: set `c := (β:ℝ)`, note `c ≠ 0` from `hβ` (`Nat.one_le_iff_ne_zero`, `Nat.cast_ne_zero`). The inner map `u t = c*(t-t₀)` has `HasDerivAt u c t₀` (`((hasDerivAt_id t₀).sub_const t₀).const_mul c`, then simplify `c*1`). Compose: `Real.cos` gives `HasDerivAt (fun t => Real.cos (u t)) (-Real.sin (u t₀) * c) t₀` via `(Real.hasDerivAt_cos (u t₀)).comp t₀ hu`; likewise `Real.sin` gives derivative `Real.cos (u t₀) * c`. At `t₀`, `u t₀ = c*(t₀-t₀) = 0`, so `Real.sin (u t₀)=0`, `Real.cos (u t₀)=1`. Assemble `szegoInterp` as `(fun t => Q₀ * cos(u t)) + (fun t => (Q₁/c) * sin(u t))` using `HasDerivAt.const_mul` and `HasDerivAt.add`; the derivative is `Q₀*(-0*c) + (Q₁/c)*(1*c) = Q₁` (the `(Q₁/c)*c = Q₁` step needs `c ≠ 0`: `div_mul_cancel₀`). You may need `HasDerivAt.congr_deriv` or `convert … using 1; …; field_simp/ring` to massage the derivative value to exactly `Q₁`. Unfold `szegoInterp` with `simp only [szegoInterp]` first if helpful.

(2) `szegoInterp_amplitude (β:ℕ) (Q₀ Q₁ t₀ : ℝ) : ∃ φ:ℝ, ∀ t, szegoInterp β Q₀ Q₁ t₀ t = Real.sqrt (Q₀^2 + (Q₁/(β:ℝ))^2) * Real.cos ((β:ℝ)*(t-t₀) - φ)`.
Let `p := Q₀`, `q := Q₁/(β:ℝ)`, `A := Real.sqrt (p^2+q^2)`. Use the complex number `z : ℂ := ⟨p, q⟩` (i.e. `Complex.mk p q`, or `(p:ℂ) + (q:ℂ)*Complex.I`). Set `φ := Complex.arg z`. Then `Complex.abs z = Real.sqrt (p^2+q^2) = A` (via `Complex.abs_apply`, `Complex.normSq_mk`/`Complex.normSq_add_mul_I`; `normSq ⟨p,q⟩ = p^2+q^2` up to `sq`/`mul_self` — use `Real.sqrt` congruence). Case `A = 0`: then `p^2+q^2 = 0` ⇒ `p=0 ∧ q=0` (`pow_eq_zero`/`add_eq_zero` with squares nonneg), so `szegoInterp … = 0 = A * _`; take `φ:=0`. Case `A ≠ 0` (so `z ≠ 0`): `Complex.cos_arg (hz : z ≠ 0) : Real.cos (arg z) = z.re / Complex.abs z` and `Complex.sin_arg : Real.sin (arg z) = z.im / Complex.abs z`, giving `Real.cos φ = p/A`, `Real.sin φ = q/A`. Then for each `t`, expand `Real.cos ((β:ℝ)*(t-t₀) - φ)` with `Real.cos_sub` = `cos(β(t-t₀))cos φ + sin(β(t-t₀))sin φ`, multiply by `A`, substitute `A*cos φ = A*(p/A) = p` and `A*sin φ = q` (needs `A≠0`, `mul_div_cancel₀`), and unfold `szegoInterp` to match `p*cos(β(t-t₀)) + q*sin(β(t-t₀))`; finish with `ring`/`field_simp`. Search Mathlib names with the lean-lsp tools (`lean_loogle`, `lean_leansearch`) to confirm exact `Complex.cos_arg`/`Complex.sin_arg`/`Complex.abs_apply` signatures before use. Keep both proofs self-contained; do not touch `szegoInterp_abs_le` (already proven from amplitude).

Use the lean-lsp MCP tools to check your proof (diagnostics/goal). When done, output a SHORT plain-text report: which decls you closed, which remain, and any blocker. No JSON required.
